author: @mikedodger7898
text:
Comments:
Good evening boys (that seems to be the local parlance, and happy birthday 🎂), 

Hello from Victoria, BC Canada. I've been a member for over a year and I love all the videos you produce.

In fact, I don't participate in many live stream events, but I'm looking forward to yours on June 6th, 7 pm.

Q1: Is is your time zone the same as London England?

Q2: How do I get into the channel? Is there a code or something else required?

Q3: Is your live stream info posted somewhere that I should check in the future?

Thanks for all the hard work and useful insights you share in all of your videos.

Most sincerely,
Mike D
Toronto, Ontario, Canada (but I'm currently in Victoria BC)

author: @mikedodger7898
text: Hey Rob, i tried to send you a note from your WorkingInk website, but the message function doesn't work.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: what was the message mate?

replies.author: @mikedodger7898
@mikedodger7898
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering For example, what happens if you get "demonitized"? If you have email addresses in your control, you can continue to offer your expertise until things can be fixed. YouTube is a great platform, but "all your eggs in one place ...".

author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text:
" This paper overcomes this barrier by constructing a class of soliton solutions that are capable of superluminal motion and sourced by purely positive energy densities. The solitons are also shown to be capable of being sourced from the stress–energy of a conducting plasma and classical electromagnetic fields. "

"Classical electromagnetic fields" - cough cough...
19:27 UTC
"While the hard-magnetic properties of tetrataenite are attractive, those demonstrated so far do not surpass those of Nd2Fe14B, although the maximum energy product of meteoritic samples may have been underestimated. The properties would be improved by increasing the degree of chemical order, as shown in the present work to be possible by annealing. Ab-initio calculations suggest that judicious alloying can increase saturation magnetization, coercivity, and magnetocrystalline anisotropy"

author: @weedfreer
text:
The volume to me sounds a wee bit distant (volume up a little or sit closer to your mic?)...also, a little echoey likely as a result of solid walls and little in the way of sound dampening.

Also, it sounds a little 'tinny' in general...are you able to adjust the sound equalisation or maybe afford a better mic?

I mean, in general, it works doesn't it.

Just, there's a couple of things that detract from the experience.

I guess, we'll just have to see you next Tuesday then.

😉

author: @J.Javid124
text: Hi Professor.  Don't worry, I follow your programs from Iran.  You are so great, I have a request,, because your viewers may not be fluent in English!  If you only talk during the program, and there is no picture or video with talking, the viewer will not understand your words!  Therefore, express the contents with photos, slides or videos. Thank you

author: @TheBaconWizard
text: I have quite a few tips on how to deaden sound in a space like that, after I did a sound-production diploma during lockdown. Scap materials can be used. But it's not an inconsiderable effort so it all depends if you'll be doing enough streaming/recording in that room to make it worthwhile. Let me know :)
